l I, Diane Nelson, declare:

2 1 1 am the Director of Employment and Employee Relations for the University of
3 ||San Francisco (“the University”). I have been employed by the University as the Director of
4 ||Employment and Employee Relations for 8 years. I make the following statements on my own
5 || personal knowledge, and if called upon, could and would testify competently thereto.

6 2. General Description of the University; University of San Francisco operates
7 || primarily out of San Francisco, Califomia. The University is a fully-accredited institution of
8 ||higher education providing undergraduate and post-graduate degree programs for national and |
9 |) mternationally-based students.

10 3. The University’s annual revenues approximate $367 million. In 2017, the
11 || University processed ovet $10 million in loans to its students, which were primarily funded
12 ||through federal government loan programs. The University enrolls and receives tuition
13 || payments from thousands of out-of-state and international students.

14 4, Of the nearly 1,500 students in the University’s 2017 freshman class,
15 ||approximately 39% came from States other than California. The University also enrolls over
16 || 1,500 international students, representing 91 different countries. The University’s admission
17 || department actively recruits prospective applicants from outside of California, employing
18 ||numerous out-of-state admission representatives.

19 5. The University offers online programs to students. from around the world, with
20 |\the University faculty and staff teaching such courses and programs. Moreover, the University
21 |loffers study abroad and exchange programs worldwide, including programs in Senegal,
22 ||Morocco, Jordan, China, India, Japan, England, France, Germany, Argentina, Brazil, among
23 |{numerous other countries.
24 6. The University’s faculty are regularly recruited from all over the United States
25 jJand the world. Many serve on out of state or international appointments or committees,
26 || frequently traveling for those appointments, as well as for other University related purposes,

' 27 |}such as academic conferences or seminars.

1 7. As the Director of Employment and Employee Relations at the University, I am
2 ||the person designated to process the paperwork for new University employees. I am readily
3 || familiar with the documents related to the Plaintiff's employment agreement (“the Agreement’)
4 |\ and serve as the University’s custodian of records for these records.
5 8. Plaintiff Robert Bascherini: The University hired Plaintiff in April of 2009 as
6 |1a student-employee in the position of front-desk attendant. In 2012, the University hired
7 || Plaintiff as the Intermural/Club Sport Director. Plaintiff executed an agreement accepting the
8 j!Intermural/Club Sport Director position on December 20, 2012 (“Agreement”). Plaintiff's
9 j}employment in that position commenced on January 2, 2013. His salary was approximately
10 || $69,720.35 for 2016-2017. The term of Plaintiff's employment concluded on May 24, 2017.
il 9. Plaintiff's Assent to the Employment Agreement: I signed and transmitted by
12 || email the proposed Agreement to Plaintiff on December 19, 2012. The Agreement included an
13. || arbitration provision. The Agreement was a two-page document. On December 20, 2012, I
14 |i received the fully executed Agreement bearing Plaintiff's signature. The University employed
15 || Plaintiff under the terms of the Agreement until the University terminated his employment on
16 || May 24, 2017.
17 10. The University’s personnel records for Plaintiff contain the mutually executed
18 || Agreement, executed by Plaintiff and dated December 20, 2012, a true and correct copy of
19 || which is attached as Exhibit A. The text of the Agreement states in relevant part:
20 “This letter contains the entire agreement between you and the University on the
terms of your employment. ... Please confirm your acceptance of this offer by
21 signing, dating, and returning this letter to the Human Resources Office. .. .”
22 11. It is the University’s standard business practice upon issuing an offer of
23 || employment to ask that the employee receiving the offer to sign the Agreement on the signature
24 ||page on the second page of the Agreement. On December 20, 2012, Plaintiff signed the
25 || signature page contained in the Agreement, a true and correct copy of which is attached as
26 || Exhibit A.
27 12. Agreement by Plaintiff to Arbitrate Disputes: The Agreement, Exhibit A,
28 || contained a one paragraph arbitration provision.

December 19, 2012
Mr. Robert Azcarate-Bascherini CWID; 20161119
. 1600 Califonia Street #41
San Francisco, CA 94109
Dear Mr, Azcarate-Bascherini:
‘The University of San Francisco is pleased to offer you the position of Intramural/Club Sports Director
within Recreational Sports/Koret, commencing omJanuary 2, 2013. This letter specifies the terms of the
offer. Intramural/Club Sports Director is an exempt position, meaning it is a salary (and not hourly)
paid position. Your semi-monthly salary will be $2,556.25 subject to all applicable withholdings and
_ deductions.
Should you accept this offer, your duties will be as described on the attached job description. You will
report to Charles White, Senior Director.
The Administrative Staff Handbook describes other terms and conditions of employment with the
University and can be referenced at http:/Avww.usfca.cdu/HR/Employment/Table_of Contents/.
You are eligible for University provided benefits, subject to the terms of policies and benefits plans,

. which may change from time to time, To schedule your Benefits Orientation please register online at
alurnniusfca.edu/benefitsorientation. Because your start date is January 2, 2013, the earliest your
medical coverage wil! begin is February 1, 2013. Delay in scheduling your benefits orientation could
delay the date of your medical coverage.

Your employment at the University will be "at will" as defined by Califomia law. The University may
conclude your emplayment for any reason or for no reason, with or without notice. Likewise, you may
conclude your employment at any time for any reason or for no reason, with or without notice. No
person other than a Vice-President of the University may change this at-will employment relationship
and such change must be made in writing and signed by the Vice-President,

Any and all legal disputes initiated by either you or the University must be submitted only to binding
neutral arbitration, before a mutually chosen arbitrator, and not to a court, judge or jury, Both parties
have the right to be represented by counsel of their choice; each is entitled to discovery consistent with
law; and to obtain those remedies for claims and defenses as would be ordered by a court. The
University will pay all of the professional fees of the arbitrator unless you object. The arbitrator will
issue a written award that must be accompanied by a written statement of decision. Legal disputes
include those arising under this contract or under federal, state and/or local statutes; the common law of
California: all contract and tort and other legal matters; but excluding unemployment benefits, workers’
compensation benefits, the Labor Management Relations Act of 1947, and EEOC, DFBH, and/or State

 
Labor Commissioner wage complaints, which complaints may be filed with the government agencies
notwithstanding this arbitration provision.
This letter contains the entire agreement between you and the University on the terms of your
employment and supersedes any and all prior or contemporaneous agreements or understandings. The
terms of your employment as stated herein will be governed by and construed under the laws of the State
of California, and may not be modified unless in writing signed by you and the University.
Please confirm your acceptance of this offer by signing, dating, and returning this letter to the Human
Resources Office, atin: Jennifer Heap.
We look forward to working with you in your new position.
Sincerely yours, ;
Diane L Nelson
Director of Employment and Employee Relations
ACCEPTED AND AGREED
T accept the conditions of employment as described above. .

Job Title: Intramural/Club Sports Director
Department: Recreational Sports/Koret ;
Position Number: G99813
Job Description
Reporting directly to the Sr. Director of the Recreational Sports Department, the Director is responsible
to oversee all aspects of Intramural Sports, Club Sports, and Equipment Desk, and Locker Room
operations, Responsibilities include compliance and implementations of comprehensive, safety
standards, budget oversight, customer service, business operations, facility upkeep, and all related
programs. This position requires excellence and effective leadership skills and outstanding supervisory
abilities to providing guidance to all relevant staff, members and participants.
As a member of the larger staff in Student Life, the Director of the Recreational Sports Department’s
Intramural/Club Sports/Equipment Desk operations collaborates and cooperates to create leadership and .
intercultural competence opportunities to support student learning and development in the Jesuit
Catholic tradition. The mission of Student Life is to fully support holistic student development within a
; social justice framework centered in preparing students to be caring, socially responsible citizens in our
global and local community.
Job Responsibilities
* Hire, supervise and evaluate Administrative Staff, Grad Assistants, Coaches, Professional .
Officials, and all student staff as relevant to this position
* Ensure that all relevant facilities maintain exceptionally high safety standards to ensure a safe
environment for all participants and that all staff are proactively trained and evaluated and all paper
work is on file for every area including club sports, travel, and game management procedures and
‘policies.
» Ensure that the above Koret programs are managed to the highest quality standards while
maintaining strong enrollments and exceptional user and participant satisfaction.
* Set policy and operating standards for all event and functions including: safety, customer
service, staff accountability, programming, member services, budgetary accountability, etc. Ensure
these objective and accountabilities are consistently met.
¢ Provide clear, consistent and effective direction and communication to all staff, participants, and
members
« Manage and oversee all business operations and systems including: administrative staff,
registration, payroll, record maintenance and staf/particlpant communication.
. * Manage and determine the facility needs including: appropriate scheduling and _
usage allocation, travel needs, etc, are met for all areas.
* Partner with USF Facilities Management to ensure areas of use are safe, proactively maintained
and in good working order. Including but not limited to two major gymnasiums, major soccer field,
combative, and boxing rooms and other areas as assigned by the Director including use rules to
insure that the collective needs of our stadent, faculty, staff, and memberships/patrons are being
served.
* Provide ongoing coaching and constructive feedback to staff. Evaluate staff per Koret

 
Policies, procedures and timeframes
* In conjunction with the Marketing Department coordinate and execute an ongoing
Program and marketing strategy for all areas of responsibility
+ Maintain ongoing communication with the Sr. Director of Recreational Sports regarding
program/facility/staff needs, challenges and successes.
* Ability to perform other duties as needed, such as coaching, teaching, instruction,
* In collaboration with USF ITS Department coordinate a computer system that integrates the newest
software designs that will enhance and improve Koret operations and services. (Banner/Class
Integrations).
* Model behavior based on RSD working principles and customer service standards.
* Complete all Koret paperwork requirements in a timely manner, including room requisitions,
payroll materials, departmental goals and work plans, budget preparation, tracking materials, catalog
copy, employment paperwork, and others as needed.
¢ Be committed to the mission and goals of USF. Participate in and promote USF events and
programs outside of own department. ;
* Additional duties as required and assigned by the Director.

Minimum Qualifications
« Minimurn of three years full time Senior Staff Program/Facility Management experience, in a

Recreational Sports Department or Similar Professional Setting.

o Directly responsible for all event operations including safety standards, staff supervision,
facility maintenance, operational and revenue budgeting, business operations, financial
performance/oversight and program development/management.

« Strong Intramural/Club Sports background including:

o Certification and CPR/AED required.

o Experience within the Fitness/Wellness Programs (Instructor Certification Optional)

o Experience within Aquatics (Ideal but not essential)

o Experience supervising a complex staff including Professional Officials, Student Staff,
Professional Coaches, Instructors, and others a3 necessary.

* Master's Degree Preferred, Bachelor Degree required, advanced degree preferred in relevant
fields (Recreation, Sport Management, etc.).

« Experience developing and managing budgets and implementing generating revenue programs.

* Strong and effective leadership qualities such as:

o Strong written and verbal communicator.

o Exceptional coaching and supervisory skills and experience.

o Proven ability to develop and inspire a large and diverse staff team.

o Professional and charismatic presence.

© Attention to detail and independently motivated.

o Self-image of personal responsibility and accountability.

o Student service focused
o Must heve outstanding team membership skills
* Outstanding computer skills, including advanced skills in MS Office and the ability to
learn new business software programs, with emphasis on Class Software/Active Network,
Banner Software and experience in the integration of both software systems. Comprehensive
understanding and operational experience with IMeagues.com —. :
¢ Ability to work in a fast paced environment and possess excellent time management skills.
